title: strychnos froesii
intro: Strychnos froesii, also known as the Froes's poison nut, is a plant species in the genus Strychnos, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Strychnos froesii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in traditional medicine, often in the form of decoctions or infusions.
